div.h1: Concurrency Models: Thread-per-Connection vs Thread Pool

div.p: Invariant: thread pool contract — exactly N threads, always, regardless of connection count. Excess connections get 503.
pthread_create() per connection Cost: ~15 µs × 1000/s = 15 ms/s overhead
N=1000 connections: 1000 threads × 8 MB = 8 GB virtual Scheduler overhead grows super-linearly
N=1000 connections: 16 threads × 8 MB = 128 MB virtual 984 connections queued or rejected pthread_create() called 16× at startup only
1000 slow connections → 1000 threads blocked forever → Server paralysed → Real clients cannot connect
[ fd₁ ][ fd₂ ][ fd₃ ] … [ fd₁₀₂₄ ] head → ← tail
1000 slow connections: → 16 worker slots filled → Queue fills → 503 Retry-After: 1 → Fast clients still served → Server continues operating
